   126→//	This is the stuff that is permanent for an AI object and is therefore saved to disk.
   127→typedef struct ai_static {
   128→	uint8_t			behavior;					// 
   129→	int8_t			flags[MAX_AI_FLAGS];		// various flags, meaning defined by constants
   130→	short			hide_segment;				//	Segment to go to for hiding.
   131→	short			hide_index;					//	Index in Path_seg_points
   132→	short			path_length;				//	Length of hide path.
   133→	int8_t			cur_path_index;			//	Current index in path.
   134→	int8_t			dying_sound_playing;		//	!0 if this robot is playing its dying sound.
   135→
   136→// -- not needed! -- 	short			follow_path_start_seg;	//	Start segment for robot which follows path.
   137→// -- not needed! -- 	short			follow_path_end_seg;		//	End segment for robot which follows path.
   138→
   139→	short			danger_laser_num;
   140→	int			danger_laser_signature;
   141→	fix			dying_start_time;			//	Time at which this robot started dying.
   142→
   143→//	int8_t			extras[28];					//	32 extra bytes for storing stuff so we don't have to change versions on disk
   144→} ai_static;
   145→
   146→//	This is the stuff which doesn't need to be saved to disk.
   147→typedef struct ai_local {
   148→//	These used to be bytes, changed to ints so I could set watchpoints on them.
   149→//	player_awareness_type..rapidfire_count used to be bytes
   150→// goal_segment used to be short.
   151→	int			player_awareness_type;	//	type of awareness of player
   152→	int			retry_count;				//	number of retries in physics last time this object got moved.
   153→	int			consecutive_retries;		//	number of retries in consecutive frames (ie, without a retry_count of 0)
   154→	int			mode;							//	current mode within behavior
   155→	int			previous_visibility;		//	Visibility of player last time we checked.
   156→	int			rapidfire_count;			//	number of shots fired rapidly
   157→	int			goal_segment;				//	goal segment for current path
   158→
   159→	// -- MK, 10/21/95, unused -- fix			last_see_time, last_attack_time;	//	For sound effects, time at which player last seen, attacked
   160→	
   161→	fix			next_action_time;			// time in seconds until something happens, mode dependent
   162→	fix			next_fire;					// time in seconds until can fire again
   163→	fix			next_fire2;					// time in seconds until can fire again from second weapon
   164→	fix			player_awareness_time;	//	time in seconds robot will be aware of player, 0 means not aware of player
   165→	fix			time_player_seen;			//	absolute time in seconds at which player was last seen, might cause to go into follow_path mode
   166→	fix			time_player_sound_attacked;			//	absolute time in seconds at which player was last seen with visibility of 2.
   167→	fix			next_misc_sound_time;			//	absolute time in seconds at which this robot last made an angry or lurking sound.
   168→	fix			time_since_processed;	//	time since this robot last processed in do_ai_frame
   169→	vms_angvec	goal_angles[MAX_SUBMODELS];	//angles for each subobject
   170→	vms_angvec	delta_angles[MAX_SUBMODELS];	//angles for each subobject
   171→	int8_t			goal_state[MAX_SUBMODELS];	// Goal state for this sub-object
   172→	int8_t			achieved_state[MAX_SUBMODELS];	// Last achieved state
   173→} ai_local;

   167→(* Effect handler *)
   168→let ai_frame_effect_handler
   169→  : type a. a Effect.t -> ((a, _) Effect.Deep.continuation -> _) option
   170→  =
   171→  fun eff ->
   172→  match eff with
   173→  | Ox_ai_frame.Ai_multiplayer_awareness agitation ->
   174→    Some
   175→      (fun k ->
   176→        let result = effect_ai_multiplayer_awareness agitation in
   177→        Effect.Deep.continue k (result <> 0))
   178→  | Ox_ai_frame.Do_ai_robot_hit_attack ->
   179→    Some
   180→      (fun k ->
   181→        effect_do_ai_robot_hit_attack ();
   182→        Effect.Deep.continue k ())
   183→  | Ox_ai_frame.Ai_fire_laser_at_player (gpx, gpy, gpz, gun_num, fpx, fpy, fpz, nf_in, nf2_in) ->
   184→    Some
   185→      (fun k ->
   186→        write_af_fire_timers nf_in nf2_in;
   187→        effect_ai_fire_laser_at_player gpx gpy gpz gun_num fpx fpy fpz;
   188→        let fs = read_af_fire_state () in
   189→        Effect.Deep.continue k (fs.(0), fs.(1), fs.(2)))
   190→  | Ox_ai_frame.Calc_gun_point gun_num ->
   191→    Some
   192→      (fun k ->
   193→        let result = effect_calc_gun_point gun_num in
   194→        Effect.Deep.continue k result)
   195→  | Ox_ai_frame.Create_path_to_player (max_length, safety_flag) ->
   196→    Some
   197→      (fun k ->
   198→        effect_create_path_to_player max_length safety_flag;
   199→        let ps = read_af_path_state () in
   200→        Effect.Deep.continue k ps)
   201→  | Ox_ai_frame.Create_path_to_station max_time ->
   202→    Some
   203→      (fun k ->
   204→        effect_create_path_to_station max_time;
   205→        let ps = read_af_path_state () in
   206→        Effect.Deep.continue k ps)
   207→  | Ox_ai_frame.Create_n_segment_path (length, avoid_seg) ->
   208→    Some
   209→      (fun k ->
   210→        effect_create_n_segment_path length avoid_seg;
   211→        let ps = read_af_path_state () in
   212→        Effect.Deep.continue k ps)
   213→  | Ox_ai_frame.Create_n_segment_path_to_door (length, avoid_seg) ->
   214→    Some
   215→      (fun k ->
   216→        effect_create_n_segment_path_to_door length avoid_seg;
   217→        let ps = read_af_path_state () in
   218→        Effect.Deep.continue k ps)
   219→  | Ox_ai_frame.Attempt_to_resume_path ->
   220→    Some
   221→      (fun k ->
   222→        effect_attempt_to_resume_path ();
   223→        let ps = read_af_path_state () in
   224→        Effect.Deep.continue k ps)
   225→  | Ox_ai_frame.Ai_follow_path (vis, prev_vis, vtpx, vtpy, vtpz) ->
   226→    Some
   227→      (fun k ->
   228→        effect_ai_follow_path vis prev_vis vtpx vtpy vtpz;
   229→        let ps = read_af_path_state () in
   230→        Effect.Deep.continue k ps)
   231→  | Ox_ai_frame.Move_towards_segment_center ->
   232→    Some
   233→      (fun k ->
   234→        effect_move_towards_segment_center ();
   235→        Effect.Deep.continue k ())
   236→  | Ox_ai_frame.Compute_vis_and_vec (vpx, vpy, vpz) ->
   237→    Some
   238→      (fun k ->
   239→        let result = effect_compute_vis_and_vec vpx vpy vpz in
   240→        Effect.Deep.continue k result)
   241→  | Ox_ai_frame.Ai_multi_send_robot_position flag ->
   242→    Some
   243→      (fun k ->
   244→        effect_ai_multi_send_robot_position flag;
   245→        Effect.Deep.continue k ())
   246→  | Ox_ai_frame.Do_boss_stuff pv ->
   247→    Some
   248→      (fun k ->
   249→        effect_do_boss_stuff pv;
   250→        Effect.Deep.continue k ())
   251→  | Ox_ai_frame.P_Rand ->
   252→    Some
   253→      (fun k ->
   254→        let result = effect_p_rand () in
   255→        Effect.Deep.continue k result)
   256→  | Ox_ai_frame.Make_random_vector ->
   257→    Some
   258→      (fun k ->
   259→        let result = effect_make_random_vector () in
   260→        Effect.Deep.continue k result)
   261→  | Ox_ai_frame.Object_to_object_visibility ->
   262→    Some
   263→      (fun k ->
   264→        let result = effect_object_to_object_visibility () in
   265→        Effect.Deep.continue k result)
   266→  | Ox_ai_frame.Do_snipe_frame (dist, vis, vtpx, vtpy, vtpz, mode) ->
   267→    Some
   268→      (fun k ->
   269→        effect_do_snipe_frame dist vis vtpx vtpy vtpz mode;
   270→        let ps = read_af_path_state () in
   271→        Effect.Deep.continue k ps)
   272→  | Ox_ai_frame.Do_escort_frame (dist, vis) ->
   273→    Some
   274→      (fun k ->
   275→        let _new_mode = effect_do_escort_frame dist vis in
   276→        let ps = read_af_path_state () in
   277→        Effect.Deep.continue k ps)
   278→  | Ox_ai_frame.Do_thief_frame (dist, vis, vtpx, vtpy, vtpz, pat, pat_time) ->
   279→    Some
   280→      (fun k ->
   281→        effect_do_thief_frame dist vis vtpx vtpy vtpz pat pat_time;
   282→        let ps = read_af_path_state () in
   283→        Effect.Deep.continue k ps)
   284→  | Ox_ai_frame.Do_any_robot_dying_frame ->
   285→    Some
   286→      (fun k ->
   287→        let result = effect_do_any_robot_dying_frame () in
   288→        Effect.Deep.continue k (result <> 0))
   289→  | Ox_ai_frame.Make_nearby_robot_snipe ->
   290→    Some
   291→      (fun k ->
   292→        effect_make_nearby_robot_snipe ();
   293→        Effect.Deep.continue k ())
   294→  | Ox_ai_frame.Move_away_from_player ->
   295→    Some
   296→      (fun k ->
   297→        effect_move_away_from_player ();
   298→        Effect.Deep.continue k ())
   299→  | Ox_ai_frame.Openable_doors_in_segment ->
   300→    Some
   301→      (fun k ->
   302→        let result = effect_openable_doors_in_segment () in
   303→        Effect.Deep.continue k result)
   304→  | Ox_ai_frame.Invalidate_escort_goal ->
   305→    Some
   306→      (fun k ->
   307→        effect_invalidate_escort_goal ();
   308→        Effect.Deep.continue k ())
   309→  | Ox_ai_frame.Laser_create_new_easy (fvx, fvy, fvz, fpx, fpy, fpz, objnum, weapon_id) ->
   310→    Some
   311→      (fun k ->
   312→        effect_laser_create_new_easy fvx fvy fvz fpx fpy fpz objnum weapon_id;
   313→        Effect.Deep.continue k ())
   314→  | Ox_ai_frame.Do_companion_extras (dist, vis, vtpx, vtpy, vtpz, mode, nf_in, nf2_in) ->
   315→    Some
   316→      (fun k ->
   317→        write_af_fire_timers nf_in nf2_in;
   318→        let nf = effect_do_companion_extras dist vis vtpx vtpy vtpz mode in
   319→        Effect.Deep.continue k nf)
   320→  | Ox_ai_frame.Do_thief_extras (dist, vis, vtpx, vtpy, vtpz, nf_in, nf2_in) ->
   321→    Some
   322→      (fun k ->

1→/*
     2→ * parity.h - Dual-execution parity testing for OCaml bridge
     3→ *
     4→ * Snapshots full game state before/after OCaml and C execution paths,
     5→ * compares field-by-field, and logs divergences. C path is canonical.
     6→ *
     7→ * Compile with USE_OX_BRIDGE to enable. Zero overhead when disabled.
     8→ *
     9→ * Runtime control:
    10→ *   OX_PARITY_ASSERT=1  - crash on first divergence
    11→ *   OX_PARITY_LOG=file  - write divergences to file
    12→ */
    13→
    14→#ifndef OX_PARITY_H
    15→#define OX_PARITY_H
    16→
    17→#ifdef USE_OX_BRIDGE
    18→
    19→#ifdef __cplusplus
    20→extern "C" {
    21→#endif
    22→
    23→#include "fix/fix.h"
    24→
    25→/* Forward declarations - actual types come from game headers */
    26→struct object;
    27→struct ai_local;
    28→struct player;
    29→struct wall;
    30→
    31→#define PARITY_MAX_OBJECTS  350
    32→#define PARITY_MAX_PLAYERS  8
    33→#define PARITY_MAX_WALLS    254
    34→#define PARITY_MAX_SEGMENTS 900
    35→
    36→typedef struct game_state_snapshot {
    37→	/* Object array - only up to Highest_object_index+1 are meaningful */
    38→	struct object   objects[PARITY_MAX_OBJECTS];
    39→	struct ai_local ai_local_info[PARITY_MAX_OBJECTS];
    40→	struct player   players[PARITY_MAX_PLAYERS];
    41→	struct wall     walls[PARITY_MAX_WALLS];
    42→
    43→	/* Per-segment object list heads (obj_relink modifies these) */
    44→	short           seg_objects[PARITY_MAX_SEGMENTS];
    45→	int             highest_segment_index;
    46→
    47→	/* Key globals */
    48→	unsigned int    rand_state;
    49→	int             highest_object_index;
    50→	int             player_is_dead;
    51→	int             player_exploded;
    52→	int             overall_agitation;
    53→	int             num_walls;
    54→	fix             frame_time;
    55→	fix             game_time;
    56→	int             frame_count;
    57→} game_state_snapshot;
    58→
    59→/*
    60→ * Snapshot the current game state into dst.
    61→ */
    62→void parity_snapshot(game_state_snapshot *dst);
    63→
    64→/*
    65→ * Restore game state from src (used between OCaml and C runs).
    66→ */
    67→void parity_restore(const game_state_snapshot *src);
    68→
    69→/*
    70→ * Compare two snapshots field-by-field.
    71→ * Logs all divergences to stderr (and optionally to file).
    72→ * Returns number of divergent fields.
    73→ *
    74→ * fn_name: which function was being compared (e.g. "do_physics_sim")
    75→ * frame: current frame number
    76→ * objnum: object being processed (-1 if N/A)
    77→ */
    78→int parity_compare(const game_state_snapshot *a, const game_state_snapshot *b,
    79→                   const char *fn_name, int frame, int objnum);
    80→
    81→/*
    82→ * Initialize parity system. Call once at startup.
    83→ * Reads OX_PARITY_ASSERT and OX_PARITY_LOG environment variables.
    84→ */
    85→void parity_init(void);
    86→
    87→/*
    88→ * Pre-allocated snapshot buffers for use in dual-execution wrappers.
    89→ * Avoids repeated allocation of ~350KB snapshots.
    90→ */
    91→extern game_state_snapshot parity_snap_before;
    92→extern game_state_snapshot parity_snap_after_ocaml;
    93→extern game_state_snapshot parity_snap_after_c;
    94→
    95→#ifdef __cplusplus
    96→}
    97→#endif
    98→
    99→#endif /* USE_OX_BRIDGE */
   100→
   101→#endif /* OX_PARITY_H */
   102→

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have all the information. Let me create a comprehensive summary.

## Summary

I found the C struct definition for **ai_local** in both Descent 1 and Descent 2, along with how it's packed for state logging. Here are the key findings:

### ai_local Struct Definition

**Location:** 
- D1: `/Users/richard/code/ChocolateDescent/main_d1/aistruct.h` (lines 134-156)
- D2: `/Users/richard/code/ChocolateDescent/main_d2/aistruct.h` (lines 147-173)

**Fields at byte offsets 4 and 8:**

For **D1** (main_d1/aistruct.h):
```c
typedef struct ai_local {
  int8_t   player_awareness_type;      // offset 0
  int8_t   retry_count;                // offset 1
  int8_t   consecutive_retries;        // offset 2
  int8_t   mode;                       // offset 3
  int8_t   previous_visibility;        // offset 4  <-- BYTE OFFSET 4
  int8_t   rapidfire_count;            // offset 5
  // ... (more fields)
} ai_local;
```

- **Byte offset 4**: `previous_visibility` (int8_t)
- **Byte offset 8**: Part of `goal_segment` (short at offset 6-7) and beginning of `last_see_time` (fix/int at offset 8)

For **D2** (main_d2/aistruct.h):
```c
typedef struct ai_local {
  int      player_awareness_type;      // offset 0-3
  int      retry_count;                // offset 4-7
  int      consecutive_retries;        // offset 8-11
  // ...
} ai_local;
```

- **Byte offset 4**: `retry_count` (int - bytes 4-7)
- **Byte offset 8**: `consecutive_retries` (int - bytes 8-11)

**Note:** D2 changed the first few fields from int8_t to full int (4 bytes) to allow setting watchpoints (see comment in D2 aistruct.h lines 148-149).

### State Logging Format

**How ai_local is packed for state logging:**

In `/Users/richard/code/ChocolateDescent/ox/input_replayer.cpp` (line 266):
```c
fwrite(Ai_local_info, sizeof(ai_local), n_obj, state_log);
```

The ai_local structures are written as raw binary dumps, one after another for each object.

   240→        Effect.Deep.continue k result)
   241→  | Ox_ai_frame.Ai_multi_send_robot_position flag ->
   242→    Some
   243→      (fun k ->
   244→        effect_ai_multi_send_robot_position flag;
   245→        Effect.Deep.continue k ())
   246→  | Ox_ai_frame.Do_boss_stuff pv ->
   247→    Some
   248→      (fun k ->
   249→        effect_do_boss_stuff pv;
   250→        Effect.Deep.continue k ())
   251→  | Ox_ai_frame.P_Rand ->
   252→    Some
   253→      (fun k ->
   254→        let result = effect_p_rand () in
   255→        Effect.Deep.continue k result)
   256→  | Ox_ai_frame.Make_random_vector ->
   257→    Some
   258→      (fun k ->
   259→        let result = effect_make_random_vector () in
   260→        Effect.Deep.continue k result)
   261→  | Ox_ai_frame.Object_to_object_visibility ->
   262→    Some
   263→      (fun k ->
   264→        let result = effect_object_to_object_visibility () in
   265→        Effect.Deep.continue k result)
   266→  | Ox_ai_frame.Do_snipe_frame (dist, vis, vtpx, vtpy, vtpz, mode) ->
   267→    Some
   268→      (fun k ->
   269→        effect_do_snipe_frame dist vis vtpx vtpy vtpz mode;
   270→        let ps = read_af_path_state () in
   271→        Effect.Deep.continue k ps)
   272→  | Ox_ai_frame.Do_escort_frame (dist, vis) ->
   273→    Some
   274→      (fun k ->
   275→        let _new_mode = effect_do_escort_frame dist vis in
   276→        let ps = read_af_path_state () in
   277→        Effect.Deep.continue k ps)
   278→  | Ox_ai_frame.Do_thief_frame (dist, vis, vtpx, vtpy, vtpz, pat, pat_time) ->
   279→    Some
   280→      (fun k ->
   281→        effect_do_thief_frame dist vis vtpx vtpy vtpz pat pat_time;
   282→        let ps = read_af_path_state () in
   283→        Effect.Deep.continue k ps)
   284→  | Ox_ai_frame.Do_any_robot_dying_frame ->
   285→    Some
   286→      (fun k ->
   287→        let result = effect_do_any_robot_dying_frame () in
   288→        Effect.Deep.continue k (result <> 0))
   289→  | Ox_ai_frame.Make_nearby_robot_snipe ->
   290→    Some
   291→      (fun k ->
   292→        effect_make_nearby_robot_snipe ();
   293→        Effect.Deep.continue k ())
   294→  | Ox_ai_frame.Move_away_from_player ->
   295→    Some
   296→      (fun k ->
   297→        effect_move_away_from_player ();
   298→        Effect.Deep.continue k ())
   299→  | Ox_ai_frame.Openable_doors_in_segment ->
   300→    Some
   301→      (fun k ->
   302→        let result = effect_openable_doors_in_segment () in
   303→        Effect.Deep.continue k result)
   304→  | Ox_ai_frame.Invalidate_escort_goal ->
   305→    Some
   306→      (fun k ->
   307→        effect_invalidate_escort_goal ();
   308→        Effect.Deep.continue k ())
   309→  | Ox_ai_frame.Laser_create_new_easy (fvx, fvy, fvz, fpx, fpy, fpz, objnum, weapon_id) ->
   310→    Some
   311→      (fun k ->
   312→        effect_laser_create_new_easy fvx fvy fvz fpx fpy fpz objnum weapon_id;
   313→        Effect.Deep.continue k ())
   314→  | Ox_ai_frame.Do_companion_extras (dist, vis, vtpx, vtpy, vtpz, mode, nf_in, nf2_in) ->
   315→    Some
   316→      (fun k ->
   317→        write_af_fire_timers nf_in nf2_in;
   318→        let nf = effect_do_companion_extras dist vis vtpx vtpy vtpz mode in
   319→        Effect.Deep.continue k nf)
   320→  | Ox_ai_frame.Do_thief_extras (dist, vis, vtpx, vtpy, vtpz, nf_in, nf2_in) ->
   321→    Some
   322→      (fun k ->
   323→        write_af_fire_timers nf_in nf2_in;
   324→        let nf = effect_do_thief_extras dist vis vtpx vtpy vtpz in
   325→        Effect.Deep.continue k nf)
   326→  | _ -> None
   327→;;

   329→(* D1 bridge function *)
   330→let cd_do_ai_frame_d1
   331→      ai_state
   332→      rinfo
   333→      frame_time
   334→      frame_count
   335→      game_time
   336→      game_mode
   337→      difficulty_level
   338→      overall_agitation
   339→      player_is_dead

   365→    (fun () ->
   366→       Ox_ai_frame.do_ai_frame_d1
   367→         ~ai_state
   368→         ~rinfo
   369→         ~frame_time
   370→         ~frame_count

   386→         ~console_y
   387→         ~console_z
   388→         ~console_size
   389→         ~believed_x
   390→         ~believed_y
   391→         ~believed_z

   393→         ~orient

   406→    }

   312→(* -- Collision sub-effect handler ----------------------------------------- *)
   313→(* Handles effects performed by Ox_collide collision functions *)
   314→
   315→let collision_effect_handler (type a) ~is_d2 (eff : a Effect.t)
   316→  : ((a, _) Effect.Deep.continuation -> _) option
   317→  =
   318→  match eff with
   319→  (* New collision effects *)
   320→  | Ox_collide.Add_points_to_score score ->
   321→    Some (fun (k : (a, _) Effect.Deep.continuation) ->
   322→      effect_add_points_to_score score;
   323→      Effect.Deep.continue k ())
   324→  | Ox_collide.Play_collision_sound (sid, seg, px, py, pz) ->
   325→    Some (fun (k : (a, _) Effect.Deep.continuation) ->
   326→      effect_play_collision_sound sid seg px py pz;
   327→      Effect.Deep.continue k ())
   328→  | Ox_collide.Create_awareness_event (objnum, atype) ->
   329→    Some (fun (k : (a, _) Effect.Deep.continuation) ->
   330→      effect_create_awareness_event objnum atype;
   331→      Effect.Deep.continue k ())
   332→  | Ox_collide.Write_back_hit_object packed ->
   333→    Some (fun (k : (a, _) Effect.Deep.continuation) ->
   334→      effect_write_back_hit_object packed;
   335→      Effect.Deep.continue k ())
   336→  | Ox_collide.Apply_damage_to_player (player_objnum, killer_objnum, damage) ->
   337→    Some (fun (k : (a, _) Effect.Deep.continuation) ->
   338→      effect_apply_damage_to_player player_objnum killer_objnum damage;
   339→      Effect.Deep.continue k ())
   340→  (* D1 robot damage effects *)
   341→  | Ox_collide.Increment_kills ->
   342→    Some (fun (k : (a, _) Effect.Deep.continuation) ->
   343→      effect_increment_kills ();
   344→      Effect.Deep.continue k ())
   345→  | Ox_collide.Start_boss_death obj_id ->
   346→    Some (fun (k : (a, _) Effect.Deep.continuation) ->
   347→      effect_start_boss_death obj_id;
   348→      Effect.Deep.continue k ())
   349→  | Ox_collide.Explode_object obj_id ->
   350→    Some (fun (k : (a, _) Effect.Deep.continuation) ->

   545→
   546→(* -- Physics sim effect handler ------------------------------------------- *)
   547→
   548→let physics_sim_effect_handler (type a) ~is_d2 (eff : a Effect.t)
   549→  : ((a, _) Effect.Deep.continuation -> _) option
   550→  =
   551→  match eff with
   552→  | Ox_physics_sim.Find_vector_intersection
   553→      (p0x, p0y, p0z, p1x, p1y, p1z, rad, thisobjnum, ignore_list, flags, startseg) ->
   554→    Some
   555→      (fun k ->
   556→        try
   557→        let query =
   558→          [| p0x
   559→           ; p0y
   560→           ; p0z
   561→           ; p1x
   562→           ; p1y
   563→           ; p1z
   564→           ; rad
   565→           ; thisobjnum
   566→           ; flags
   567→           ; startseg
   568→           ; Array.length ignore_list
   569→          |]
   570→        in
   571→        let packed = Array.append query ignore_list in
   572→        let result = effect_find_vector_intersection packed in
   573→        (* Unpack: fate, hit_pnt xyz, hit_seg, hit_side, hit_side_seg,
   574→           hit_object, wallnorm xyz, n_segs, seglist[] *)
   575→        let fate = result.(0) in
   576→        let hit_px = result.(1) in
   577→        let hit_py = result.(2) in
   578→        let hit_pz = result.(3) in
   579→        let hit_seg = result.(4) in
   580→        let hit_side = result.(5) in
   581→        let hit_side_seg = result.(6) in
   582→        let hit_object = result.(7) in
   583→        let wn_x = result.(8) in
   584→        let wn_y = result.(9) in
   585→        let wn_z = result.(10) in
   586→        let n_segs = result.(11) in
   587→        let seglist = Array.sub result ~pos:12 ~len:n_segs in
   588→        Effect.Deep.continue
   589→          k
   590→          ( fate
   591→          , hit_px
   592→          , hit_py
   593→          , hit_pz
   594→          , hit_seg
   595→          , hit_side
   596→          , hit_side_seg
   597→          , hit_object
   598→          , wn_x
   599→          , wn_y
   600→          , wn_z
   601→          , n_segs
   602→          , seglist )
   603→        with Invalid_argument _ ->
   604→          Printf.eprintf "OX_SHADOW: FVI exception, returning HIT_NONE\n";
   605→          Out_channel.flush stderr;
   606→          (* Return HIT_NONE with dummy values *)
   607→          Effect.Deep.continue k (0, p0x, p0y, p0z, startseg, 0, -1, -1, 0, 0, 0, 0, [||]))
   608→  | Ox_physics_sim.Collide_object_with_wall
   609→      (hit_speed, wall_seg, wall_side, hit_px, hit_py, hit_pz, obj_flags) ->
   610→    Some
   611→      (fun k ->
   612→        let result =
   613→          effect_collide_object_with_wall
   614→            hit_speed
   615→            wall_seg
   616→            wall_side
   617→            hit_px
   618→            hit_py
   619→            hit_pz
   620→            obj_flags
   621→        in
   622→        Effect.Deep.continue k result)
   623→  | Ox_physics_sim.Scrape_object_on_wall (wall_seg, wall_side, hit_px, hit_py, hit_pz) ->
   624→    Some
   625→      (fun k ->
   626→        let result =
   627→          effect_scrape_object_on_wall wall_seg wall_side hit_px hit_py hit_pz
   628→        in
   629→        Effect.Deep.continue k result)
   630→  | Ox_physics_sim.Collide_two_objects (hit_objnum, pos_hit_x, pos_hit_y, pos_hit_z) ->
   631→    Some
   632→      (fun k ->
   633→        (* Fetch collision data from C for both objects *)
   634→        let cd = effect_fetch_collision_data hit_objnum in
   635→        (* Try OCaml dispatch *)
   636→        let dispatch_fn =
   637→          if is_d2 then Ox_collide.collide_two_objects_d2
   638→          else Ox_collide.collide_two_objects_d1
   639→        in
   640→        let ocaml_result =
   641→          Effect.Deep.match_with
   642→            (fun () -> dispatch_fn ~cd ~hit_px:pos_hit_x ~hit_py:pos_hit_y ~hit_pz:pos_hit_z)
   643→            ()
   644→            { retc = (fun x -> x)
   645→            ; exnc = raise
   646→            ; effc = (fun (type b) (eff : b Effect.t) ->
   647→                collision_effect_handler ~is_d2 eff)
   648→            }
   649→        in
   650→        let result =
   651→          match ocaml_result with
   652→          | Some (flags, vx, vy, vz, shields) ->
   653→            effect_write_back_this_object shields flags;
   654→            (flags, vx, vy, vz)
   655→          | None ->
   656→            (* Fall back to C for unported collision types *)
   657→            effect_collide_two_objects hit_objnum pos_hit_x pos_hit_y pos_hit_z
   658→        in
   659→        Effect.Deep.continue k result)
   660→  | Ox_physics_sim.Obj_relink (objnum, new_seg) ->
   661→    Some
   662→      (fun k ->
   663→        effect_obj_relink objnum new_seg;
   664→        Effect.Deep.continue k ())
   665→  | Ox_physics_sim.Find_object_seg objnum ->
   666→    Some
   667→      (fun k ->
   668→        let result = effect_find_object_seg objnum in
   669→        Effect.Deep.continue k result)
   670→  | Ox_physics_sim.Update_object_seg objnum ->
   671→    Some
   672→      (fun k ->
   673→        effect_update_object_seg objnum;
   674→        Effect.Deep.continue k ())
   675→  | Ox_physics_sim.Find_point_seg (px, py, pz, seg) ->
   676→    Some
   677→      (fun k ->
   678→        let result = effect_find_point_seg px py pz seg in
   679→        Effect.Deep.continue k result)
   680→  | Ox_physics_sim.Get_seg_masks (px, py, pz, seg) ->
   681→    Some
   682→      (fun k ->
   683→        let result = effect_get_seg_masks px py pz seg in
   684→        Effect.Deep.continue k result)
   685→  | Ox_physics_sim.Compute_segment_center seg ->
   686→    Some
   687→      (fun k ->
   688→        let result = effect_compute_segment_center seg in
   689→        Effect.Deep.continue k result)
   690→  | Ox_physics_sim.Add_stuck_object (wall_seg, wall_side) ->
   691→    Some
   692→      (fun k ->
   693→        effect_add_stuck_object wall_seg wall_side;
   694→        Effect.Deep.continue k ())
   695→  | Ox_physics_sim.Find_connect_side (seg1, seg2) ->
   696→    Some
   697→      (fun k ->
   698→        let result = effect_find_connect_side seg1 seg2 in
   699→        Effect.Deep.continue k result)
   700→  | Ox_physics_sim.Wall_is_doorway (seg, side) ->
   701→    Some
   702→      (fun k ->
   703→        let result = effect_wall_is_doorway seg side in
   704→        Effect.Deep.continue k result)
   705→  | Ox_physics_sim.Create_abs_vertex_lists_and_dist (seg, side, spx, spy, spz) ->
   706→    Some
   707→      (fun k ->
   708→        let result = effect_create_abs_vertex_lists_and_dist seg side spx spy spz in
   709→        Effect.Deep.continue k result)
   710→  | Ox_physics_sim.Tmap_is_force_field (seg, side) ->
   711→    Some
   712→      (fun k ->
   713→        let result = effect_tmap_is_force_field seg side in
   714→        Effect.Deep.continue k result)
   715→  | Ox_physics_sim.Vm_vector_2_matrix_orient (vx, vy, vz, ux, uy, uz) ->
   716→    Some
   717→      (fun k ->
   718→        let result = effect_vm_vector_2_matrix_orient vx vy vz ux uy uz in
   719→        Effect.Deep.continue k result)
   720→  | Ox_physics_sim.Query_object_type_id objnum ->
   721→    Some
   722→      (fun k ->
   723→        (* Reuse collision data fetch — hit object type at index 20, id at 21 *)
   724→        let cd = effect_fetch_collision_data objnum in
   725→        Effect.Deep.continue k (cd.(20), cd.(21)))
   726→  | _ -> None
   727→;;
   728→
   729→(* D1 bridge function *)
   730→let cd_do_physics_sim_d1
   731→      pos_x
   732→      pos_y

1→(* FVI bridge — v2 on-demand effects entry points.
     2→   All FVI functions use algebraic effects (Fetch_segment_data, Fetch_object_data,
     3→   Fetch_collision_table) to fetch data on demand from C, avoiding O(world) copying. *)
     4→
     5→(* check_trans_wall callback: OCaml calls back into C for per-pixel
     6→   transparency check on FQ_TRANSPOINT walls.
     7→   Args: segnum, sidenum, facenum, hit_x, hit_y, hit_z → 1 if passable *)
     8→external check_trans_wall_c
     9→  :  int
    10→  -> int
    11→  -> int
    12→  -> int
    13→  -> int
    14→  -> int
    15→  -> int
    16→  = "cd_ox_check_trans_wall_bytecode" "cd_ox_check_trans_wall"
    17→
    18→let () = Ox_fvi.check_trans_wall_callback := check_trans_wall_c
    19→
    20→(* C externals for on-demand data fetching *)
    21→external fetch_segment_data_c : int -> int array = "cd_ox_fetch_segment_data"
    22→external fetch_object_data_c : int -> int array = "cd_ox_fetch_object_data"
    23→external fetch_collision_table_c : unit -> int array = "cd_ox_fetch_collision_table"
    24→
    25→(* Run an OCaml function under effect handlers for FVI data fetching.
    26→   Handles: Fetch_segment_data, Fetch_object_data, Fetch_collision_table *)
    27→let run_with_fvi_effects : 'a. (unit -> 'a) -> 'a =
    28→  fun f ->
    29→  Effect.Deep.match_with f ()
    30→    { retc = (fun x -> x)
    31→    ; exnc = (fun e -> raise e)
    32→    ; effc = (fun (type a) (eff : a Effect.t) ->
    33→        match eff with
    34→        | Ox_gameseg.Fetch_segment_data segnum ->
    35→          Some (fun (k : (a, _) Effect.Deep.continuation) ->
    36→            let data = fetch_segment_data_c segnum in
    37→            Effect.Deep.continue k data)
    38→        | Ox_fvi.Fetch_object_data objnum ->
    39→          Some (fun (k : (a, _) Effect.Deep.continuation) ->
    40→            let data = fetch_object_data_c objnum in
    41→            Effect.Deep.continue k data)
    42→        | Ox_fvi.Fetch_collision_table ->
    43→          Some (fun (k : (a, _) Effect.Deep.continuation) ->
    44→            let data = fetch_collision_table_c () in
    45→            Effect.Deep.continue k data)
    46→        | _ -> None)
    47→    }
    48→;;
    49→
    50→let cd_find_vector_intersection_v2 (arr : int array) =
    51→  try
    52→    run_with_fvi_effects (fun () -> Ox_fvi.find_vector_intersection_v2 arr)
    53→  with exn ->
    54→    Printf.eprintf "OX_SHADOW: cd_find_vector_intersection_v2 exception: %s\n"
    55→      (Exn.to_string exn);
    56→    Out_channel.flush stderr;
    57→    let p0x = arr.(0) in
    58→    let p0y = arr.(1) in
    59→    let p0z = arr.(2) in
    60→    [| 3; p0x; p0y; p0z; arr.(3); 0; -1; 0; 0; 0; 0; 0 |]
    61→;;
    62→
    63→let cd_object_intersects_wall_v2 (arr : int array) =
    64→  try
    65→    run_with_fvi_effects (fun () -> Ox_fvi.sphere_intersects_wall_v2 arr)
    66→  with _ -> 0
    67→;;
    68→

   121→  Callback.register "cd_object_intersects_wall_v2" cd_object_intersects_wall_v2;
   122→  Callback.register "cd_find_homing_object_complete_v2" cd_find_homing_object_complete_v2;
   123→  Callback.register "cd_find_homing_object_v2" cd_find_homing_object_v2;
   124→  Callback.register "cd_track_track_goal_v2" cd_track_track_goal_v2;
   125→  Callback.register "cd_player_is_visible_from_object_v2" cd_player_is_visible_from_object_v2;

## **FVI Bridge (fvi_bridge.ml) — 4 Core Data Effects**

These use **on-demand data fetching** to avoid O(world) copying:

| Effect Name | C Function | Purpose |
|---|---|---|

**Note:** FVI functions (`find_vector_intersection_v2`, `compute_vis_and_vec_v2`, `player_is_visible_from_object_v2`, etc.) all use these 4 effects internally. Each FVI call triggers multiple fetch operations.

---

## **Highest Priority Candidates for Porting**

Based on call frequency and impact:

### **Tier 1 (Most Frequent, High Impact)**
1. **Ai_multiplayer_awareness** (47 calls) — Core AI decision-making
2. **Ai_multi_send_robot_position** (41 calls) — Multiplayer sync (skip if single-player focus)
3. **Create_path_to_player** (11 calls) — Path planning core
4. **Compute_vis_and_vec** (4 calls) — Visibility checks (heavy FVI user)
5. **Compute_segment_center** (10 calls) — Physics navigation

### **Tier 2 (Medium Frequency, Moderate Impact)**
6. **Create_n_segment_path** (9 calls) — Path planning variant
7. **Obj_relink** (8 calls) — Object spatial management
8. **Find_vector_intersection** — Core raycasting (used by FVI, physics)
9. **Find_object_seg** (4 calls) — Object location
10. **Create_n_segment_path_to_door** (6 calls) — Path planning variant

### **Tier 3 (Specific Behaviors)**
11. **Calc_gun_point** (3 calls) — Weapon aiming
12. **Do_boss_stuff** (3 calls) — Boss logic
13. **Do_any_robot_dying_frame** — Death animations
14. **Collide_object_with_wall** — Wall collision response
15. **Scrape_object_on_wall** — Wall sliding

---

## **Key Insights**

1. **Path Planning Cluster:** `create_path_*`, `create_n_segment_path*`, `attempt_to_resume_path`, `ai_follow_path` (~40 calls total) — These are tightly coupled and should be ported as a unit.

2. **FVI Bottleneck:** `Compute_vis_and_vec` triggers on-demand segment/object/collision fetches (3 effects per call). Porting FVI would eliminate ~4+ ms of pure bridging overhead per visibility check.

3. **Physics Core:** `Find_vector_intersection`, `Collide_object_with_wall`, `Scrape_object_on_wall` are fundamental. Porting these together would improve physics consistency.

4. **Multiplayer:** `Ai_multi_send_robot_position` (41 calls) is frequent but can be skipped if targeting single-player parity first.

5. **Collision Dispatch:** `collide_two_objects_d1/d2` in `Ox_collide` already handles robot+robot, player+clutter, player+weapon, etc. The fallback to C's `collide_two_objects()` is for unported collision pair types (robot+weapon, weapon+wall, etc.).

---

**Total External Effects Across All Bridges: 103 unique C function call sites**

The highest ROI porting targets are **Ai_multiplayer_awareness**, **path creation/following functions**, and **FVI-related visibility checks**, as these are called most frequently during gameplay.

I can see several differences between C and OCaml in the retry/consecutive_retries handling:

1. **AIM_GOTO_PLAYER** (C line 978-983): The C code calls `move_towards_segment_center` then `create_path_to_player(obj, 100, 1)`. The OCaml code doesn't handle `aim_goto_player` at all! There's no match case for it.

2. **AIM_GOTO_OBJECT** (C line 985-993): C sets `Escort_goal_object = ESCORT_GOAL_UNSPECIFIED`. OCaml doesn't handle this case either.


3. **AIM_STILL** (C line 998-1002): C checks `robptr->attack_type` and either moves toward the segment center or checks behavior, but OCaml skips the attack_type check and only evaluates behavior without the `aib_follow` condition.

4. **AIM_BEHIND** (C line 1020-1026): C simply moves toward the segment center and zeroes velocity, whereas OCaml adds conditional path creation based on agitation and difficulty — a completely different behavior that doesn't match the C implementation.

Actually, looking at the actual code more carefully, the C version for AIM_BEHIND is straightforward: it moves toward the segment center and clears all velocity components. The OCaml version does move toward the segment center but then branches on agitation level to conditionally create a path to the player, which is fundamentally different from what C does.

For AIM_STILL, the C code checks the attack_type flag before deciding whether to move toward the segment center or attempt to resume the path, but the OCaml version is missing that attack_type check entirely and also doesn't account for the aib_follow behavior. I need to verify whether these are intentional D2 differences or actual bugs in the OCaml translation. I've identified three distinct bugs in the OCaml code: AIM_BEHIND creates paths instead of zeroing velocity like C does, AIM_STILL is missing both the attack_type check and aib_follow in its behavior validation, and AIM_GOTO_PLAYER/AIM_GOTO_OBJECT cases are completely absent. Since I should fix one bug at a time, I'm leaning toward AIM_BEHIND since it could cascade into multiple divergences through the physics system, though I need to decide if that's actually the root cause of the retry_count divergence at frame 16 or if it's something else entirely.

input.prompt:
Search ox/ox_ai_frame.ml for potential OCaml operator precedence bugs involving `land`, `lor`, `lxor`, `lsr`, `asr`. In OCaml, these bitwise operators have different precedence than comparison operators. Common bugs:

1. `x land y = z` means `x land (y = z)` in OCaml (wrong!), should be `(x land y) = z`
2. `x land y <> 0` means `x land (y <> 0)` in OCaml (wrong!), should be `(x land y) <> 0`  
3. `x lor y` in boolean contexts where `||` was intended

Look for patterns like:
- `something land something = something` (without parens around the land)

Now I have found the issues. Let me compile my findings:

## Bug Report: OCaml Operator Precedence Issues in ox/ox_ai_frame.ml

I found **ONE CRITICAL BUG** and several instances of the correct pattern:

### CRITICAL BUG (Line 1750):
```ocaml
if !phys_flags land pf_uses_thrust <> 0
```
**This is buggy!** In OCaml, `land` has lower precedence than `<>`, so this parses as:
```ocaml
if !phys_flags land (pf_uses_thrust <> 0)
```
This is semantically wrong. It should be:
```ocaml
if (!phys_flags land pf_uses_thrust) <> 0
```

The intention is to check if the `pf_uses_thrust` bit is set in `!phys_flags`, but instead it's doing a bitwise AND between `!phys_flags` and the *boolean result* of `pf_uses_thrust <> 0`.

### FALSE POSITIVES (Correct patterns):
The following lines are **correctly parenthesized** and do NOT have bugs:

1. **Lines 896, 1793** - `player_flags land player_flags_cloaked = 0`
   - Correctly uses parens in the overall expression

2. **Lines 912, 928, 960, 1129, 1190, 1332, 1412, 1832, 1850, 1887, 2030, 2218, 2271, 2417, 2516** - `game_mode land gm_multi = 0`
   - All correctly structured

3. **Lines 1012, 1433, 1941, 2535** - `obj_ref land 3 = 0`
   - All correctly structured

4. **Lines 269, 859, 942, 1019, 1050, 1150, 1170, 1426, 1727, 1750, 1871, 1953, 2241, 2257, 2296, 2449, 2531** - `... land ... <> 0`
   - Wait, line 1750 appears in this list too

5. **Lines 1170, 2257** - `obj_ref land 1 <> 0`
   - These are correctly parenthesized as part of larger boolean AND expressions

6. **Line 2183** - `frame_count land 0x0f = 0`
   - Correctly structured

### Summary:
**File:** `/Users/richard/code/ChocolateDescent/ox/ox_ai_frame.ml`
**Line 1750:** Operator precedence bug - missing parentheses around `land`

```ocaml
# BUGGY (line 1750):
if !phys_flags land pf_uses_thrust <> 0

# SHOULD BE:
if (!phys_flags land pf_uses_thrust) <> 0
```

         if !behavior = aib_station then anger_level := 64;

           if
             maybe_ai_do_actual_firing_stuff

               ~goal_state:!goal_state

               ~current_state:!current_state
           then (
             compute_vis ();
             do_actual_firing ()))
         else (
           let vtpx, vtpy, vtpz = !vec_to_player in
           unpack_path_state (Effect.perform
             (Ai_follow_path (!player_visibility, saved_prev_vis, vtpx, vtpy, vtpz)));
           if !goal_state <> ais_flin
           then goal_state := ais_lock
           else if !current_state = ais_flin
           then goal_state := ais_lock;
           if !behavior <> aib_run_from
           then do_firing_stuff ();
           if
             !player_visibility = 2
             && !behavior <> aib_snipe
             && !behavior <> aib_follow
             && !behavior <> aib_run_from
             && obj_id <> robot_brain
             && companion = 0
             && thief = 0
           then (if attack_type = 0 then mode := aim_chase_object)
           else if
             !player_visibility = 0
             && !behavior = aib_normal
             && !mode = aim_follow_path
             && !dist_to_player > f1_0 * (20 * (2 * difficulty_level + pursuit))
             && game_time - !time_player_seen > f1_0 / 2 * (difficulty_level + pursuit)
           then (
             mode := aim_still;
             hide_index := -1;
             path_length := 0);
           Effect.perform (Ai_multi_send_robot_position (-1)))
       | m when m = aim_behind ->
         let ok = Effect.perform (Ai_multiplayer_awareness 71) in
         if not ok
         then (
           if
             maybe_ai_do_actual_firing_stuff
               ~game_mode

               ~current_state:!current_state

             compute_vis ();
             do_actual_firing ()))

              is handled by the bridge post-OCaml section when player_visibility=2 *)

           then goal_state := ais_lock
           else if !current_state = ais_flin
           then goal_state := ais_lock;
           Effect.perform (Ai_multi_send_robot_position (-1)))
       | m when m = aim_still ->
         if
           !dist_to_player < (f1_0 * 120) + (difficulty_level * f1_0 * 20)
           || !player_awareness_type >= pa_weapon_robot_collision - 1
         then (
           compute_vis ();
           if
             !player_visibility = 2

           then (

           do_firing_stuff ();
           if !player_visibility = 2
           then
             if attack_type = 1
             then (
               behavior := aib_normal;
               let ok = Effect.perform (Ai_multiplayer_awareness 80) in
               if not ok
               then maybe_fire_mp ()

               then (
                 Effect.perform (Ai_multi_send_robot_position 1);
                 ai_evaded := 0)
               else Effect.perform (Ai_multi_send_robot_position (-1)))

               then maybe_fire_mp ()
               else if !ai_evaded <> 0
               then (
                 Effect.perform (Ai_multi_send_robot_position (-1));
                 ai_evaded := 0)
               else Effect.perform (Ai_multi_send_robot_position (-1)))
           else if
             obj_segnum <> !hide_segment
             && !dist_to_player > f1_0 * 80
             && game_mode land gm_multi = 0
           then
             if !behavior = aib_station
             then (
               goal_segment := !hide_segment;
               unpack_path_state (Effect.perform (Create_path_to_station 15))))
       | m when m = aim_open_door ->
         let ok = Effect.perform (Ai_multiplayer_awareness 62) in
         if ok then Effect.perform (Ai_multi_send_robot_position (-1))
       | m when m = aim_snipe_wait || m = aim_snipe_retreat ->
         (* Snipe wait/retreat: no firing in mode dispatch (C lines 1699-1705) *)
         ()
       | m
         when m = aim_snipe_retreat_backwards
              || m = aim_snipe_attack
              || m = aim_snipe_fire ->
         (* Snipe retreat_backwards/attack/fire: firing in mode dispatch (C lines 1706-1715) *)
         let ok = Effect.perform (Ai_multiplayer_awareness 53) in
         if ok then do_actual_firing ()
       | m when m = aim_thief_attack || m = aim_thief_retreat || m = aim_thief_wait ->
         (* Thief modes handled by Do_thief_frame effect above *)
         ()
       | _ -> mode := aim_chase_object);

`object_animates` affects firing logic — it determines whether the robot needs to wait for animation to reach the fire state before actually firing. OK I was wrong! In C's AIS_FIRE case (lines 1827-1844), `ai_do_actual_firing_stuff` IS called at line 1842, OUTSIDE the `if (player_visibility == 2)` block. So C DOES call it unconditionally for AIS_FIRE. OCaml also calls it unconditionally at line 2533. These match.

But wait, there's a subtlety. In C (line 1830):```c
if (player_visibility == 2) {```C checks `player_visibility == 2` (not just > 0). In OCaml (line 2525):```ocaml


if !player_visibility > 0 then (```OCaml checks `player_visibility > 0`. These are different! C only enters the visibility branch when player_visibility is 2 (fully visible), while OCaml enters when player_visibility is 1 OR 2 (any visibility).

This means:
- When player_visibility = 1, C skips the ai_turn/awareness/send block, while OCaml enters it
- When player_visibility = 0, both skip it
- When player_visibility = 2, both enter it

This IS a bug! In OCaml, when player_visibility = 1, it would execute code that C skips entirely, which is a behavioral divergence between the two implementations. , the OCaml version checks `> 0` while C checks `== 2` in multiple places—AIS_SRCH and AIS_RECO both show this same discrepancy. But since single-player mode treats these multiplayer functions as no-ops anyway, these differences don't actually cause observable divergences during parity testing. The real issue surfaces in AIS_LOCK where the logic branches differently depending on whether it's multiplayer or not. For the AIS_FIRE state, there's a meaningful difference: C only fires when `player_visibility == 2`, while OCaml fires when `player_visibility > 0`. In single player where visibility is 1, C skips firing entirely while OCaml would proceed, but since OCaml is also missing the actual firing logic, both end up with no functional difference in practice. The awareness effect in single player just returns 1 with no side effects, so these visibility threshold differences don't actually cause functional divergences. Now I'm examining the AIS_FIRE case more closely—in single player, the awareness check returns true, which skips the multiplayer-only firing code and falls through to the standard firing logic.

Comparing the two implementations, the C version executes the turn-towards-vector call, sends a position update (which is a no-op in single player), then does the actual firing. The OCaml version appears to follow a similar path but I need to verify where it branches.

Looking at the actual divergences between D1 and D2, the most impactful fix would be correcting the `object_animates` calculation in the D1 do_ai_frame function—those AIM_BEHIND and AIM_STILL cases in the retry section are clearly wrong. file.content:
(* do_silly_animation: compute goal and delta angles for robot animation joints.
   C original: main_d1/ai.cpp + main_d2/ai2.cpp do_silly_animation
   D1 and D2 are identical.

   Packed input array layout:
     [0]   num_guns
     [1]   flinch_attack_scale (pre-computed: 1, Attack_scale=24, or Flinch_scale=4)
     [2]   model_n_models (for jointnum bounds check)
     [3..3+num_guns]  n_joints_per_gun[g] for g = 0..num_guns  (num_guns+1 entries)

     Then joint entries, grouped by gun, each 4 ints:
       jointnum, target_p, target_b, target_h

     Then current anim_angles for joints 0..model_n_models-1:
       model_n_models * 3 ints (p, b, h)

     Then current goal_angles for joints 0..model_n_models-1:
       model_n_models * 3 ints (p, b, h)

     Then current delta_angles for joints 0..model_n_models-1:
       model_n_models * 3 ints (p, b, h)

     Then current achieved_state[0..num_guns]: (num_guns+1) ints
     Then current goal_state[0..num_guns]: (num_guns+1) ints

   Returns packed array:
     [0]  at_goal (0 or 1, if 1 caller sets CURRENT_STATE = GOAL_STATE)
     Then goal_angles for joints 0..model_n_models-1: model_n_models * 3 ints
     Then delta_angles for joints 0..model_n_models-1: model_n_models * 3 ints
     Then achieved_state[0..num_guns]: (num_guns+1) ints
     Then goal_state[0..num_guns]: (num_guns+1) ints *)
let do_silly_animation (packed : int array) =
  let anim_rate = f1_0 / 16 in
  let delta_ang_scale = 16 in
  let ais_reco = 6 in
  let ais_fire = 5 in
  let ais_flin = 4 in
  let ais_lock = 3 in
  let num_guns = packed.(0) in
  let flinch_attack_scale = packed.(1) in
  let model_n_models = packed.(2) in
  let n_guns_plus_1 = num_guns + 1 in
  (* Read n_joints per gun *)
  let n_joints_per_gun = Array.init n_guns_plus_1 ~f:(fun g -> packed.(3 + g)) in
  (* Joint entries start after n_joints_per_gun *)
  let joints_base = 3 + n_guns_plus_1 in
  let total_joints =
    let sum = ref 0 in
    Array.iter n_joints_per_gun ~f:(fun n -> sum := !sum + n);
    !sum
  in
  (* Current anim/goal/delta angles base offsets *)
  let anim_base = joints_base + (total_joints * 4) in
  let goal_base = anim_base + (model_n_models * 3) in
  let delta_base = goal_base + (model_n_models * 3) in
  let achieved_base = delta_base + (model_n_models * 3) in
  let gstate_base = achieved_base + n_guns_plus_1 in
  (* Mutable copies of goal_angles, delta_angles, achieved_state, goal_state *)
  let goal_angles =
    Array.init (model_n_models * 3) ~f:(fun i -> packed.(goal_base + i))

This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Analysis:
Let me analyze the conversation chronologically:

1. The user's system prompt sets up a ChocolateDescent project context - porting C game logic to OCaml function by function, with parity testing between C-ref and OCaml builds.

2. The user provides parity test results showing 650,526 divergences over 6,796 frames (rate 9572, no regression from previous). The task is to make FORWARD PROGRESS by either fixing a bug in OCaml code or porting an unported C function.

3. The absolute rules state: no C→OCaml state syncing, no modifying bridge files (bridge.c, bridge.h, ai_frame_bridge.ml, main_d*/ai.cpp). Changes must go in ox/ox_*.ml.

4. My investigation path:
   - Identified earliest divergence: frame 16, ai_local[55] byte 4 (retry_count), byte 8 (consecutive_retries)
   - Found these are D2 ai_local fields: byte 4 = retry_count (int), byte 8 = consecutive_retries (int)
   - Traced retry_count to physics sim - OCaml physics produces different retry count than C physics
   - Determined this is a cascading effect from FVI/physics differences between OCaml and C builds
   
5. Investigated many potential targets:
   - ai_multiplayer_awareness: no-op in single player, won't reduce divergences
   - make_random_vector: not actually called in ox_ai_frame.ml
   - calc_gun_point: already ported to OCaml
   - compute_segment_center: needs vertex data not available
   - ai_follow_path: very complex, needs Point_segs global
   - create_path_to_player: complex graph traversal
   - ai_turn_towards_vector: already ported in ox_physics.ml but can't write orientation back without bridge changes
   - do_silly_animation: already ported in ox_physics.ml but needs animation data not passed to do_ai_frame
   - object_to_object_visibility: simple but would need FVI effect nesting

6. Found several bugs in D1 code (AIM_BEHIND wrong logic, AIM_STILL missing attack_type check and aib_follow) but we're testing D2

7. Found D2 issues:
   - AIM_BEHIND/AIM_RUN_FROM_OBJECT missing velocity zeroing (can't fix without bridge changes)
   - object_animates incorrectly set to 1 for robots with 0 guns (minor)
   - AIS_FIRE/AIS_SRCH/AIS_RECO check `player_visibility > 0` instead of `== 2` (but no effect in single player)
   - Missing ai_turn_towards_vector calls (known limitation, can't add without bridge changes)
   - do_silly_animation/ai_frame_animation never called in authoritative path (can't fix without bridge changes)

8. The agent sub-agent incorrectly claimed a precedence bug at line 1750 (`!phys_flags land pf_uses_thrust <> 0`) but OCaml's `land` has higher precedence than `<>`, so the code is correct.

9. No concrete fix was applied. The investigation was still ongoing when the summary was requested.

The key finding is that most divergences are cascading effects from:
- FVI/physics differences between OCaml and C implementations
- Animation functions not being called in the authoritative path
- Missing motion functions (ai_turn_towards_vector, ai_move_relative_to_player)
All of these either can't be fixed without bridge changes or are expected divergences from unported functions.

The user's message was just the parity test data with instructions to find and fix something. No additional user messages were sent during the conversation.

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   The user (automated parity testing loop) provided D2 parity test results showing 650,526 divergences over 6,796 frames (rate 9572/frame, same as previous — no regression). The task is to make FORWARD PROGRESS by either:
   - (A) Fixing a BUG in existing OCaml code (wrong logic, off-by-one, missing case, ordering error)
   - (B) Porting an unported C function to pure OCaml in ox/ox_*.ml
   
   Absolute rules: NEVER modify bridge.c, bridge.h, ai_frame_bridge.ml, or main_d*/ai.cpp. NEVER add C→OCaml state syncing. Changes go in ox/ox_*.ml only. ONE fix per iteration.

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - ChocolateDescent: source port of Descent 1 & 2 with incremental OCaml rewrite via algebraic effects bridge
   - OCaml-authoritative mode: do_ai_frame, do_physics_sim, find_vector_intersection are OCaml-driven
   - Parity testing: C-ref build (USE_OX_BRIDGE=OFF) vs OCaml build (USE_OX_BRIDGE=ON), comparing state logs
   - ai_local struct layout (D2): byte 0=player_awareness_type(int), byte 4=retry_count(int), byte 8=consecutive_retries(int), byte 12=mode(int), byte 56=time_since_processed(fix)
   - Object struct: bytes 20-28=pos, bytes 32-64=orient, bytes 96-104=velocity
   - Fixed-point math: F1_0 = 0x10000
   - Effects system: OCaml performs effects → bridge handler calls C external → C function runs
   - OCaml operator precedence: `land` has HIGHER precedence than `=`/`<>` (same level as `*`)

3. Files and Code Sections:
   - `main_d2/aistruct.h`: D2 ai_local struct definition — fields are full ints (not int8_t like D1). byte 4=retry_count, byte 8=consecutive_retries
   - `main_d2/physics.cpp` (lines 935-1458): C physics sim with retry_count logic. `count` starts at 0, increments in collision loop, `Ai_local_info[objnum].retry_count = count-1` after loop
   - `ox/ox_physics_sim.ml` (lines 260-665, 800-1235): OCaml D1/D2 physics sim. retry_count tracked as ref, returned as `retry_count - 1` if > 0
   - `main_d2/ai.cpp` (lines 700-1877): D2 do_ai_frame C code — OCaml-authoritative path at top (lines 718-787), C fallback path below
   - `ox/ox_ai_frame.ml`: Main OCaml AI frame implementation
     - Lines 156-230: Effect type definitions and state indices
     - Lines 680-786: D1 do_ai_frame (has bugs in AIM_BEHIND and AIM_STILL retry handling)
     - Lines 1487-2553: D2 do_ai_frame
     - Lines 1567-1590: D2 compute_vis helper
     - Lines 1596-1607: D2 unpack_path_state helper
     - Lines 1612-1654: D2 pack_result helper (ai_state_size + 7 elements)
     - Lines 1720-1730: D2 do_firing_stuff helper
     - Lines 1850-1884: D2 retry_count/consecutive_retries handling (correct)
     - Lines 1961-1968: D2 animation phase — object_animates set to 1 when dist<100, 0 otherwise
     - Lines 2196-2439: D2 mode dispatch (AIM_CHASE_OBJECT, AIM_FOLLOW_PATH, AIM_BEHIND, AIM_STILL, etc.)
     - Lines 2453-2545: D2 state transition and state machine phases
   - `main_d2/ai2.cpp`:
     - Lines 537-583: ai_turn_towards_vector — already has OCaml bridge (cd_ox_ai_turn_towards_vector)
     - Lines 780-885: do_silly_animation — already calls OCaml via cd_ox_do_silly_animation when USE_OX_BRIDGE
     - Lines 2060-2139: compute_vis_and_vec — OCaml bridge path
     - Lines 2277-2306: move_towards_segment_center
     - Lines 2932-2947: ai_multiplayer_awareness — returns 1 in single player (no-op)
   - `ox/ox_physics.ml`:
     - Line 257: calc_gun_point (already ported)
     - Line 340: ai_turn_towards_vector (already ported as pure OCaml)
     - Line 1303: do_silly_animation (already ported as pure OCaml)
   - `ox/ai_frame_bridge.ml`: Effect handlers for AI frame effects (NOT to be modified)
   - `ox/physics_bridge.ml`: Physics effect handlers including do_silly_animation callback registration

4. Errors and fixes:
   - Sub-agent incorrectly identified a precedence bug at ox_ai_frame.ml line 1750 (`!phys_flags land pf_uses_thrust <> 0`). Analysis showed OCaml's `land` has HIGHER precedence than `<>`, so the code is actually correct — `(a land b) <> 0`.
   - No code changes were made during this session.

5. Problem Solving:
   - Traced earliest divergence (frame 16, ai_local[55] byte 4) to physics retry_count difference — OCaml physics collision loop runs different number of iterations than C due to FVI/collision handling differences. This is an expected cascade from the OCaml FVI implementation.
   - Identified that do_silly_animation and ai_frame_animation are NEVER called in the OCaml-authoritative path (C's do_ai_frame returns at line 787 before reaching animation code at line 1136). This likely causes massive ai_local divergences in goal_angles/delta_angles (bytes 60+) and object anim_angles (offset 192+). Cannot fix without bridge changes.
   - Identified that ai_turn_towards_vector is already ported to OCaml but not called from do_ai_frame because orientation can't be written back through the result array without bridge changes.
   - Found minor bug: object_animates incorrectly set to 1 for all close robots in D2 do_ai_frame (line 1965), should be 0 for robots with n_guns=0. Impact is minimal.
   - Found D2 AIS_FIRE/AIS_SRCH/AIS_RECO state machine checks `player_visibility > 0` instead of C's `== 2`, but no functional impact in single player.
   - Found D2 AIM_BEHIND/AIM_RUN_FROM_OBJECT missing velocity zeroing in retry section, but can't fix without adding velocity to result array (bridge change).

6. All user messages:
   - Single user message containing parity test results and instructions: divergence data (650,526 total, rate 9572), first 50 divergent lines, absolute rules about no state syncing and no bridge modifications, instructions to identify earliest divergence, determine if bug or unported function, fix it, build, and commit. ONE fix per iteration.

7. Pending Tasks:
   - Find and implement ONE concrete fix (bug fix or function port) that reduces divergences
   - Build: `cd ox && sh ../scripts/ox/build_bridge.sh && cd .. && cmake --build build-ox -j8 --target headless_replay`
   - Commit with descriptive message
   - Do NOT run the replay (the loop does that)

8. Current Work:
   I was in the middle of an extensive investigation to find a bug to fix or function to port that would reduce D2 parity divergences. The investigation covered:
   - The earliest divergence (retry_count from physics) — cascading, not a direct bug
   - All major AI effect functions — most are either already ported, too complex, or can't be fixed without bridge changes
   - The do_silly_animation/ai_frame_animation gap — biggest divergence source but blocked by bridge rules
   - Multiple potential bugs — all either don't affect D2 or don't affect single-player parity
   
   The most recent line of investigation was looking at `do_physics_sim_rot` to see if it's properly ported, and generally searching for any remaining bug or portable function. No code changes have been made yet.

9. Optional Next Step:
   The most promising remaining avenue is to fix the minor `object_animates` bug for D2: in ox/ox_ai_frame.ml around line 1964-1968, change `object_animates := 1` to `object_animates := (if n_guns > 0 then 1 else 0)` to match C's do_silly_animation returning 0 for robots with 0 guns. This is a clear type (A) bug fix, though with minimal divergence impact. Alternatively, continue investigating the physics_sim or FVI code for subtle bugs that could cause the frame 16 retry_count divergence. if !retry_count > 0 && game_mode land gm_multi = 0
      then (
        consecutive_retries := !consecutive_retries + !retry_count;
        retry_count := 0;
        if !consecutive_retries > 3
        then (
          (match !mode with
           | m when m = aim_goto_player ->
             Effect.perform Move_towards_segment_center;
             unpack_path_state (Effect.perform (Create_path_to_player (100, 1)))
           | m when m = aim_goto_object ->
             Effect.perform Invalidate_escort_goal
           | m when m = aim_chase_object ->
             unpack_path_state (Effect.perform
               (Create_path_to_player (4 + (overall_agitation / 8) + difficulty_level, 1)))
           | m when m = aim_still ->
             if attack_type <> 0
             then Effect.perform Move_towards_segment_center
             else if not (!behavior = aib_still || !behavior = aib_station || !behavior = aib_follow)
             then unpack_path_state (Effect.perform Attempt_to_resume_path)
           | m when m = aim_follow_path ->
             if game_mode land gm_multi <> 0
             then mode := aim_still
             else unpack_path_state (Effect.perform Attempt_to_resume_path)
           | m when m = aim_run_from_object ->
             Effect.perform Move_towards_segment_center;
             unpack_path_state (Effect.perform (Create_n_segment_path (5, -1)));
             mode := aim_run_from_object
           | m when m = aim_behind ->
             Effect.perform Move_towards_segment_center
           | m when m = aim_open_door ->
             unpack_path_state (Effect.perform (Create_n_segment_path_to_door (5, -1)))
           | _ -> ());
          consecutive_retries := 0))
      else consecutive_retries := !consecutive_retries / 2;
      (* Phase: robotmaker exit — C-only line 1066-1071: if in robotmaker segment
         AND station is enabled, follow path to exit (vis=1, prev=1) and return. *)
      if game_mode land gm_multi = 0 && seg_special = segment_is_robotmaker
         && seg_station_enabled <> 0
      then (
        if objnum = 28 && game_time > 270000 && game_time < 280000 then (
          Printf.eprintf "OX28_ROBOTMAKER_EXIT gt=%d\n" game_time; flush stderr);
        unpack_path_state (Effect.perform (Ai_follow_path (1, 1, 0, 0, 0)));
        raise Early_return);
      (* Phase: awareness decay *)
      if !player_awareness_type > 0
      then
        if !player_awareness_time > 0
        then (
          player_awareness_time := !player_awareness_time - frame_time;
          if !player_awareness_time <= 0
          then (
            player_awareness_time := f1_0 * 2;
            player_awareness_type := !player_awareness_type - 1))
        else (
          player_awareness_type := !player_awareness_type - 1;
          player_awareness_time := f1_0 * 2)
      else goal_state := ais_rest;
      (* Phase: dead player *)
      if player_is_dead <> 0 && !player_awareness_type = 0
      then
        if !dist_to_player < f1_0 * 200
        then (
          let pr = Effect.perform P_Rand in
          if pr < frame_time / 8
          then
            if !behavior <> aib_still && !behavior <> aib_run_from
            then (
              let ok = Effect.perform (Ai_multiplayer_awareness 30) in
              if ok
              then (
                Effect.perform (Ai_multi_send_robot_position (-1));
                if not (!mode = aim_follow_path && !cur_path_index < !path_length - 1)
                then
                  if !behavior <> aib_snipe && !behavior <> aib_run_from
                  then
                    if !dist_to_player < f1_0 * 30
                    then unpack_path_state (Effect.perform (Create_n_segment_path (5, 1)))
                    else unpack_path_state (Effect.perform (Create_path_to_player (20, 1))))));
      (* Phase: collision awareness + visibility agitation *)
      if objnum = 28 && game_time > 270000 && game_time < 280000 then (
        Printf.eprintf "OX28_VISAGIT pat=%d prev_vis=%d obj_ref=%d dist=%d gt=%d\n"
          !player_awareness_type !previous_visibility obj_ref !dist_to_player game_time;
        flush stderr);
      if
        !player_awareness_type = pa_weapon_robot_collision
        || !player_awareness_type >= pa_player_collision
      then (
        compute_vis ();
        if !player_visibility = 1 then player_visibility := 2)
      else if
        obj_ref land 3 = 0
        && !previous_visibility = 0
        && !dist_to_player < f1_0 * 100
      then (
        let rval = Effect.perform P_Rand in
        let sval = !dist_to_player * (difficulty_level + 1) / 64 in
        if objnum = 28 && game_time > 270000 && game_time < 280000 then (
          Printf.eprintf "OX28_RAND rval=%d sval=%d fm=%d dist=%d prev=%d result=%d headlight=%d gt=%d\n"
            rval sval frame_time !dist_to_player !previous_visibility

      (match !mode with
       | m when m = aim_chase_object ->

         let circle_distance =
           if attack_type <> 1
           then circle_distance + ((objnum land 0xf) * f1_0 / 2)
           else circle_distance
         in
         compute_vis ();
         let chase_break = ref false in
         if !player_visibility < 2 && saved_prev_vis = 2

           let ok = Effect.perform (Ai_multiplayer_awareness 53) in

           then maybe_fire_mp ()

             Effect.perform (Ai_multi_send_robot_position (-1))))
         else if
           !player_visibility = 0
           && !dist_to_player > f1_0 * 80

           if !behavior = aib_station

             goal_segment := !hide_segment;

                   goal_state := ais_srch;

               ignore circle_distance;
               (* ai_move_relative_to_player on C side *)
               if obj_ref land 1 <> 0 && (!goal_state = ais_srch || !goal_state = ais_lock)
               then ();
               if !ai_evaded <> 0
               then (
                 Effect.perform (Ai_multi_send_robot_position 1);
                 ai_evaded := 0)
               else Effect.perform (Ai_multi_send_robot_position (-1));
               do_firing_stuff ())))
       | m when m = aim_run_from_object ->
         compute_vis ();
         if !player_visibility > 0
         then
           if !player_awareness_type = 0
           then player_awareness_type := pa_weapon_robot_collision;
         if game_mode land gm_multi = 0 || !player_visibility > 0
         then (
           let ok = Effect.perform (Ai_multiplayer_awareness 75) in
           if ok
           then (
             let vtpx, vtpy, vtpz = !vec_to_player in
             unpack_path_state (Effect.perform
               (Ai_follow_path (!player_visibility, saved_prev_vis, vtpx, vtpy, vtpz)));
             Effect.perform (Ai_multi_send_robot_position (-1))));
         if !goal_state <> ais_flin
         then goal_state := ais_lock
         else if !current_state = ais_flin
         then goal_state := ais_lock;
         if !next_fire <= 0 && !player_visibility > 0
         then (
           let ok = Effect.perform (Ai_multiplayer_awareness 75) in
           if ok
           then (
             let fvx = -orient.(6) in
             let fvy = -orient.(7) in
             let fvz = -orient.(8) in
             let fpx, fpy, fpz =
               Ox_math.vm_vec_add ~a:(obj_x, obj_y, obj_z) ~b:(fvx, fvy, fvz)
             in
             let weapon_id =
               if !sub_flags land sub_flags_sprox <> 0
               then superprox_id_d2
               else proximity_id_d2
             in
             Effect.perform
               (Laser_create_new_easy
                  (fvx, fvy, fvz, fpx, fpy, fpz, objnum, weapon_id));
             next_fire := (f1_0 / 2) * (ndl + 5 - difficulty_level)))
       | m when m = aim_goto_player || m = aim_goto_object ->
         (* C-only passes player_visibility=2 (hardcoded) for goto modes *)
         let vtpx, vtpy, vtpz = !vec_to_player in
         unpack_path_state (Effect.perform
           (Ai_follow_path (2, saved_prev_vis, vtpx, vtpy, vtpz)))
       | m when m = aim_follow_path ->
         let anger_level = ref 65 in
         if !behavior = aib_station then anger_level := 64;
         compute_vis ();
         let ok = Effect.perform (Ai_multiplayer_awareness !anger_level) in
         if not ok
         then (
           if
             maybe_ai_do_actual_firing_stuff
               ~game_mode
               ~goal_state:!goal_state
               ~obj_id
               ~current_state:!current_state
           then (
             compute_vis ();
             do_actual_firing ()))
         else (
           let vtpx, vtpy, vtpz = !vec_to_player in
           unpack_path_state (Effect.perform
             (Ai_follow_path (!player_visibility, saved_prev_vis, vtpx, vtpy, vtpz)));
           if !goal_state <> ais_flin
           then goal_state := ais_lock
           else if !current_state = ais_flin
           then goal_state := ais_lock;
           if !behavior <> aib_run_from
           then do_firing_stuff ();
           if
             !player_visibility = 2
             && !behavior <> aib_snipe
             && !behavior <> aib_follow
             && !behavior <> aib_run_from
             && obj_id <> robot_brain
             && companion = 0
             && thief = 0
           then (if attack_type = 0 then mode := aim_chase_object)
           else if
             !player_visibility = 0
             && !behavior = aib_normal
             && !mode = aim_follow_path
             && !dist_to_player > f1_0 * (20 * (2 * difficulty_level + pursuit))
             && game_time - !time_player_seen > f1_0 / 2 * (difficulty_level + pursuit)
           then (
             mode := aim_still;
             hide_index := -1;
             path_length := 0);
           Effect.perform (Ai_multi_send_robot_position (-1)))
       | m when m = aim_behind ->
         let ok = Effect.perform (Ai_multiplayer_awareness 71) in
         if not ok
         then (
           if
             maybe_ai_do_actual_firing_stuff
               ~game_mode
               ~goal_state:!goal_state
               ~obj_id
               ~current_state:!current_state
           then (
             compute_vis ();
             do_actual_firing ()))
         else (
           compute_vis ();
           (* Movement (move_towards_vector, ai_turn_towards_vector, ai_do_actual_firing_stuff)
              is handled by the bridge post-OCaml section when player_visibility=2 *)
           if !goal_state <> ais_flin
           then goal_state := ais_lock
           else if !current_state = ais_flin
           then goal_state := ais_lock;
           Effect.perform (Ai_multi_send_robot_position (-1)))
       | m when m = aim_still ->
         if
           !dist_to_player < (f1_0 * 120) + (difficulty_level * f1_0 * 20)
           || !player_awareness_type >= pa_weapon_robot_collision - 1
         then (
           compute_vis ();
           if
             !player_visibility = 2
             || saved_prev_vis = 2
           then (
             let ok = Effect.perform (Ai_multiplayer_awareness 71) in
             if not ok
             then maybe_fire_mp ()
             else Effect.perform (Ai_multi_send_robot_position (-1)));
           do_firing_stuff ();
           if !player_visibility = 2
           then
             if attack_type = 1
             then (
               behavior := aib_normal;
               let ok = Effect.perform (Ai_multiplayer_awareness 80) in
               if not ok
               then maybe_fire_mp ()
               else if !ai_evaded <> 0
               then (
                 Effect.perform (Ai_multi_send_robot_position 1);
                 ai_evaded := 0)
               else Effect.perform (Ai_multi_send_robot_position (-1)))
             else (
               let ok = Effect.perform (Ai_multiplayer_awareness 81) in
               if not ok
               then maybe_fire_mp ()
               else if !ai_evaded <> 0
               then (
                 Effect.perform (Ai_multi_send_robot_position (-1));
                 ai_evaded := 0)
               else Effect.perform (Ai_multi_send_robot_position (-1)))
           else if
             obj_segnum <> !hide_segment
             && !dist_to_player > f1_0 * 80
             && game_mode land gm_multi = 0
           then
             if !behavior = aib_station
             then (
               goal_segment := !hide_segment;
               unpack_path_state (Effect.perform (Create_path_to_station 15))))
       | m when m = aim_open_door ->
         let ok = Effect.perform (Ai_multiplayer_awareness 62) in
         if ok then Effect.perform (Ai_multi_send_robot_position (-1))
       | m when m = aim_snipe_wait || m = aim_snipe_retreat ->
         (* Snipe wait/retreat: no firing in mode dispatch (C lines 1699-1705) *)
         ()
       | m
         when m = aim_snipe_retreat_backwards
              || m = aim_snipe_attack
              || m = aim_snipe_fire ->
         (* Snipe retreat_backwards/attack/fire: firing in mode dispatch (C lines 1706-1715) *)
         let ok = Effect.perform (Ai_multiplayer_awareness 53) in
         if ok then do_actual_firing ()
       | m when m = aim_thief_attack || m = aim_thief_retreat || m = aim_thief_wait ->
         (* Thief modes handled by Do_thief_frame effect above *)
         ()
       | _ -> mode := aim_chase_object);
      (* Save mode-case visibility before catch-all compute_vis.
         C-only's movement code runs within the mode case, using player_visibility
         as set by compute_vis_and_vec in that mode case (or -1 if not called).
         The bridge needs this value for movement dispatch, not the catch-all result. *)

      (* Phase: final visibility *)

content.thinking:
Now let me look at the AIM_STILL case in the OCaml code more carefully vs the C code. I see a potential issue.

In C's AIM_STILL case (lines 1619-1682):
1. Check distance/awareness condition
2. compute_vis_and_vec
3. Check `(player_visibility == 2) || (previous_visibility == 2)` → turn towards, multi awareness 71, return on fail
4. do_firing_stuff (ALWAYS within the outer if)
5. Check `player_visibility == 2` for attack_type 1 vs else branches


6. Else check station/segment

In OCaml's AIM_STILL (lines 2377-2422):
1. Check distance/awareness condition ✓
2. compute_vis ✓
3. Check `player_visibility = 2 || saved_prev_vis = 2` → multi awareness 71, return on fail ✓
4. But I'm not seeing do_firing_stuff called in the same place—it looks like the OCaml version might be missing this call or it's structured differently than the C version.

Looking at the actual line ordering in both versions, in C the do_firing_stuff happens at line 1637, then the player_visibility == 2 check follows at line 1638. But in OCaml, the structure seems inverted or the call is placed elsewhere. Let me trace through the OCaml code more carefully to see where do_firing_stuff actually gets invoked in the control flow. I'm noticing a discrepancy in how the OCaml version handles the failure case compared to the C code. When `ai_multiplayer_awareness` fails in C, it triggers the full firing logic, but in OCaml I'm only calling `maybe_fire_mp()` without the actual firing sequence that should follow. I need to trace through what the C code does in that failure branch to make sure the OCaml translation captures the complete behavior. I'm noticing the OCaml version is missing a crucial condition—it sets anger_level to 64 whenever behavior is station, but the C code only does this when the final path segment matches the hide segment. This is a bug, though it only affects multiplayer since anger_level is only used there. Let me trace through the single-player code path to find issues that actually matter.
